DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-29-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Andrew R. Catterall, Esq. of Zacks, Freedman & Patterson, T:415.956.8100, for plaintiff/cross-defendant and moving party;
No other appearance.

RULING: There being no objection, the Court adopts the tentative ruling.

CROSS-DEFENDANT EASTWOOD DEVELOPMENT INCCORPORATED'S DEMURRER to the fourth and fifth causes of action in the cross-complaint filed by Robert McKee is sustained without leave to amend as to the fourth cause of action for violation of Civil Code 3344 and overruled as to the fifth cause of action for common law appropriation of name. Mr. McKee does not, nor does it appear that he can, allege that Eastwood used his name and/or contractor's license on or in any product, merchandise or good or for the purpose of advertising, selling or soliciting the purchase of a product, merchandise, good or service, which is required for section 3344 liability. Mr. McKee has adequately alleged all of the elements of a claim for common law appropriation of name. (CACI 1803).

ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Not reported.

Off Calendar Jan-04-2018 Set For Trial On Jul-23-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

OBJECTION HEARING CALENDAR
Off Calendar Jan-17-2018 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-22-2017 Continued To Jan-17-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-13-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Andrew R. Catterall of the law offices of Zacks Freedman & Patterson (415-956-8100) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Michael Fox of the law offices of Triano & Byrne (510-548-8081) appears as counsel for defendant/demurring party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT ROBERT MCKEE D.B.A. MCKEE ELECTRIC'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT as to the first cause of action for intentional misrepresentation in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Eastwood Development, Inc. is OVERRULED. Liberally construed, the first cause of action adequately alleges that McKee Electric is vicariously liable for the alleged misrepresentations of Steven McKee because those misrepresentations were made by Steven in the course and scope of his employment by McKee Electric. Since Robert McKee's demurrer is made in his capacity as the sole proprietor of McKee Electric, the first cause of action is adequately alleged against him because Robert McKee's liability and McKee Electric's liability are co-extensive. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR No. 6576, e-mail: rhunter@sftc.org. (L&M302)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-22-2017 Continued To Dec-06-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-07-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Mike Fox of the law offices of Triano & Byrne (510-548-8081) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

No other appearance is made in this matter

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS ROBERT MCKEE, STEVEN MCKEE, and ROBERT MCKEE D.B.A. MCKEE ELECTRIC's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT as to the first three causes of action alleged in the complaint filed by plaintiff Eastwood Development, Inc. is: a) as to Steven, overruled as to the first cause of action, SUSTAINED without leave to amend as to the second cause of action, and sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the third cause of action and b) as to Robert, sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the first cause of action, overruled as to the second cause of action and sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the third cause of action. The first cause of action for intentional misrepresentation is adequately alleged against Steven McKee. The claim is alleged with sufficient specificity because the complaint states facts showing Steven made oral representations to Eastwood's owner during contract negotiations that he was a licensed electrician, that his company would be the party contracting to perform the work, and that he would personally perform or oversee the work. It cannot be determined as a matter of law that Eastwood's reliance on the alleged misrepresentations were unreasonable due to Robert's signature and license number on the contracts. Eastwood failed to adequately allege a claim for intentional misrepresentation against Robert under the theories of alter ego and conspiracy. There is no allegation McKee Electric is a corporation for the alter ego doctrine to apply. There are also insufficient facts that Stephen and Robert conspired to defraud Eastwood because there is no specific evil or unlawful purpose alleged. (117 Sales Corp. v. Olsen (1978) 80 Cal.App.3d 645, 649.) The second cause of action for breach of contract fails as to Stephen because there is no allegation that Stephen entered into the contracts. There is no reasonable possibility that amendment would cure these deficiencies. The breach of contract claim is adequately alleged against Robert. The claim is not uncertain because the complaint explicitly states that the parties entered into three written contracts, which are attached to the complaint. The third cause of action for negligence is inadequately alleged against both Steven and Robert because the facts alleged do not show that defendants owed a legal duty to Eastwood independent of any contractual obligations. (Aas v. Superior Court (2000) 24 Cal.4th 627, 643.) ("A person may not ordinarily recover in tort for the breach of duties that merely restate contractual obligations.") There is also no allegation that Eastwood suffered injury to its own property. The facts currently alleged only show damage to property that Eastwood worked on as a general contractor. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Not Reported. (D302)
